summary: First test run. Backend 8/11 tests pass. Two backend bugs found: (1) GET /api/datasets/{id} returns 500 due to ObjectId leak (user_id not serialized in get_dataset response), which cascades and blocks the dataset detail page in the UI (shows 'Something went wrong' toast). (2) Brute-force lockout is broken because the identifier is IP:email and Kubernetes ingress load-balances requests across multiple source IPs (10.208.128.9 and 10.208.128.10 observed), so the per-IP counter never reaches 5. All other backend endpoints work: auth register/login/me/logout, datasets list/seed/delete, EDA, clean, feature-engineering, train (5 models), predict, forecast, prediction+model PDF reports, AI insight (Claude Sonnet 4.5), admin analytics/users/datasets/predictions and RBAC. Frontend: landing, register, login, dashboard, sidebar nav (all data-testids present), theme toggle, and seed-sample all render/fire correctly, but dataset detail page cannot render because of bug (1).

critical.issue: Returns 500: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable. Root cause: _serialize() in server.py only pops _id but not user_id. list_datasets pops user_id explicitly, but get_dataset does not. Fix: in get_dataset after _serialize(d), do out.pop('user_id', None) — or update _serialize to convert/remove user_id.

Brute-force lockout bypass. login_attempts.identifier = f'{request.client.host}:{email}'. Behind Kubernetes ingress, requests from the same client arrive with different upstream pod IPs (observed 10.208.128.9 and 10.208.128.10 alternating), so the counter never accumulates past a few for a single identifier. Result: after 6 wrong logins we saw counts of {IP1: 2, IP2: 4} — never 5, so 429 never fires. Fix: key on email alone (or use X-Forwarded-For and trust proxy headers). Documented behavior in review_request (5 fails => 15 min lock) is NOT enforced.

ui_bugs.issue: Page shows only a spinner and two 'Something went wrong. Please try again.' toasts. Downstream of backend bug #1 — no tabs (Preview/EDA/Correlation/Trends/Cleaning/Features) can render. Once GET /api/datasets/{id} is fixed, retest.

integration_issues.flow: View dataset details → EDA / Train / Predict entry points from dataset card

issues: Recharts warning: container width(-1) and height(-1) — ResponsiveContainer has no explicit min height; chart still renders but throws console warnings on initial mount.

action_items: Fix GET /api/datasets/{id}: pop or stringify user_id before returning (e.g., out.pop('user_id', None) in get_dataset, or make _serialize also handle user_id). This unblocks all UI dataset-detail flows.
Fix brute-force lockout: change identifier to just the email (or add proper X-Forwarded-For handling with a trusted proxy). Currently 5-attempt lockout is bypassed by ingress load balancer.
Optional: give Recharts ResponsiveContainer a minHeight to silence width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on dashboard chart.
After backend fixes, retest: DatasetDetail tabs, Train run, Predict flow, PDF downloads, AI Insight button, 7-day forecast chart, and admin page navigation via UI.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _serialize() is inconsistent — some routes manually pop user_id (list_datasets, admin_datasets, history) while others (get_dataset, insights response) rely on _serialize which does not. Centralize this in _serialize to prevent recurrence.
Brute-force keying by request.client.host is unreliable behind any proxy/ingress. Either key by email only or read X-Forwarded-For (and configure trusted-proxies on uvicorn).
server.py is 720 lines and mixes auth, datasets, ML, reports, admin — consider splitting into routers per domain (auth_router, datasets_router, ml_router, admin_router).
predict_from_model / train_all: no timeout guard — if a huge CSV is uploaded, request can hang. Consider background task or size cap.
CORS is added AFTER app.include_router(api); functionally OK with Starlette but order-sensitive middlewares (e.g., session) added later would not apply — worth noting.

success_rate.frontend: ~70% (landing/auth/dashboard OK; dataset detail broken by backend)

test_credentials: admin@aqi.io / admin123 (verified working). New TEST_user_* accounts created via /api/auth/register for isolation.
seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed users + one seed-sample dataset per test run (Sample_India_AQI_180days.csv, 1440 rows). Not cleaned up (delete test skipped due to earlier failure).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest with 2 xdist workers by default per pytest.ini). Two failing tests are test_seed_and_flow (500 at GET /api/datasets/{id}) and test_lockout (429 never returned). After the main agent fixes both, re-run: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -v`. For UI retest, focus on /dataset/:id tabs, /train run, /predict flow, PDF downloads, and AI Insight button. Frontend URL: https://aqi-predict-5.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (dataset 500): server.py:298 get_dataset — after `out = _serialize(d)`, `out['user_id']` is still an ObjectId; FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize it and raises ValueError -> 500. Reproduce: login, seed-sample, GET /api/datasets/{returned_id}. Fix: `out.pop('user_id', None)` right after _serialize (or fix _serialize globally). Bug 2 (lockout): server.py:141 identifier includes `request.client.host`. Behind Kubernetes ingress, client host is the upstream pod IP which round-robins. Verified in MongoDB login_attempts collection: same email has separate counters for 10.208.128.9 and 10.208.128.10. Fix: identifier = email only, or trust X-Forwarded-For.
